title: The HUF Startup Protocol: Carrier First, Observables Second, Roots Third
author: ChatGPT (formalized), Claude (verified), Peter Higgins (directed)
origin: ChatGPT proposed the A-B-C framework in response to Peter's request for 'a simple basic first steps to developing a huf system by working backwards from the data structure to the field elements.' Peter's one-line summary: 'defined root system, find roots after first finding carrier.' Claude verified alignment with the Backblaze empirical record.
pll_status: Methodological — no empirical claims. Portable across all HUF domains.
classification: Binding process document. This protocol governs how any new HUF domain is brought online.
the_motto: Do not ask how many roots the system has until you can prove what the carrier is.

the_wrong_order.description: Theory → Roots → Control. This produces architecture without evidence. The Grok routing (GRA-001) demonstrated the failure mode: extensive theoretical scaffolding around root counts without computing on real data.
the_wrong_order.failure_mode: Premature structural commitment. Importing a root count from another domain (e.g. declaring Ramsar 'six-rooted' because Backblaze was) violates PLL.

the_right_order.description: Data structure → Drift/Complexity → Empirical root map. This is what Backblaze did, and it is what every new domain must do.

step_A_find_the_carrier.title: A — Find the Carrier
step_A_find_the_carrier.instruction: Before anything else, identify the thing in the field that naturally becomes a ratio-state vector.
step_A_find_the_carrier.the_test: Can you write the system as a nonnegative vector that sums to 1? If not, you do not have a HUF object yet.
step_A_find_the_carrier.what_this_means: Choose one real field substrate. Normalize it. Check simplex validity. Name the field elements. This is CL-01 in plain language: valid HUF starts with a real simplex carrier, not an invented score.
rules: Pick ONE substrate. Do not mix area shares with budget shares with attention shares in the same first-pass analysis.
Verify the vector sums to 1 (or can be normalized to do so without distortion).
Name every field element. The carrier is not anonymous — each component has an identity.
If the carrier is unclear, STOP. Do not proceed to Step B.

examples.backblaze: Carrier = drive model share vector within a datacenter. Each component is the fraction of total drives belonging to model i. Named field elements: ST8000NM0055, WDC WD60EFRX, etc.
examples.ramsar: Carrier = site area share vector within a national portfolio. Each component is the fraction of total Ramsar area belonging to site i. Named field elements: Crna Mlaka, Lonjsko Polje, etc.
examples.planck: Carrier = to be determined. Candidate: power spectral density distribution across angular multipole bands, or pixel intensity distribution across sky regions. The carrier definition IS the research question for Step A.
examples.general: Any system where a finite set of named elements holds shares of a conserved total.

step_A_find_the_carrier.identity_note: In governance domains (Ramsar, treaty systems), identity retention matters. A portfolio can keep similar shape while silently substituting one element for another. Step A must include an identity registry, not just a shape vector.

step_B_measure_drift_and_structure.title: B — Baseline: Drift, Complexity, Identity
step_B_measure_drift_and_structure.instruction: Once the carrier exists, measure three things before inventing any architecture.

B1_drift.what: Track drift between snapshots using a simple scale-invariant distance.
B1_drift.default_metric: Total Variation (TV) distance — the CL-02 house default.
B1_drift.why_TV: Parameter-free, bounded [0,1], interpretable as maximum probability of misclassification. Backblaze PLL validated TV as the best temporal monitor after discovering the Keff_fill paradox (normalized fill can improve mechanically when carrier count falls).

B2_complexity.what: Track effective complexity via Shannon-Keff (N1 = exp(H)).
B2_complexity.default_metric: Shannon effective number N1 — the CL-03 canonical observable.
B2_complexity.why_N1: ROOT-001 showed N1 is the representative of the largest metric cluster (13 metrics at r>0.90). It is the single best summary of how many elements are effectively present.

B3_identity.what: Track whether the same field elements are still present.
checks: Same element roster? Any additions or removals?
Any elements silently dropped from the accounting?
Any elements substituted by others of similar share?
B3_identity.why: Scalar shape metrics cannot detect substitution. A 3-site portfolio can look identical to a different 3-site portfolio. Identity retention is a separate audit layer, not another scalar root.

step_B_measure_drift_and_structure.what_this_gives_you: A real HUF baseline. Is the carrier valid? Is it drifting? Is it concentrating? Are the same elements still present? This is enough to begin responsible monitoring. You do not need roots, amplifier math, or CL-05 to start.
step_B_measure_drift_and_structure.minimum_panel_for_step_C: Step B should accumulate enough snapshots to make correlation analysis meaningful. For Backblaze, 130 days across 8 quarters was sufficient. The minimum depends on domain volatility — more volatile systems need fewer snapshots, more stable systems need more.

step_C_discover_the_root_structure.title: C — Compression: Discover Roots from Data
step_C_discover_the_root_structure.instruction: After you have a real panel of snapshots, THEN test for roots. Do not begin by declaring a root count.

the_process.C1_broad_metric_library: Compute many candidate metrics on every snapshot. ROOT-001 computed 44. The list should include: uniformity/departure metrics (TV, Gini, JS, Hellinger, KL, KS), log-tail metrics (Atkinson(1), GE(0)), effective complexity (Shannon Keff, participation ratio, HHI), variance-concentration (GE(2), top-k shares, chi-squared), shape (skewness, kurtosis), richness (element count N0, singleton count), dominant-share (max share, Rényi q→∞). More is better at this stage.
the_process.C2_correlation_clustering: Build the full metric-by-metric correlation matrix across all snapshots. Cluster at multiple thresholds (r > 0.95, 0.90, 0.80, 0.70). See which metrics collapse onto the same root.
the_process.C3_eigenvalue_decomposition: Run PCA or eigenvalue analysis on the metric panel. How many dimensions capture 95%? 99%? Where is the noise floor?
the_process.C4_polarity_test: THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT STEP. For each candidate root, test whether higher or lower values correspond to 'healthier' in the domain. RCA-001 proved that polarity is first-order and dimensionality is second-order. Without correct polarity, more roots makes things WORSE.
the_process.C5_structural_vs_operational_split: Separate roots that measure distribution shape from roots that measure outcomes. Backblaze found failure rate and failure count as operational roots completely decorrelated from all structural metrics. Do not force outcome variables into the structural clustering.
the_process.C6_minimum_commitment: Commit only to the smallest empirically justified root set. Do not add roots for theoretical completeness. Each root must earn its place by being (a) empirically independent, (b) polarity-tested, and (c) decision-relevant.

what_not_to_do: Do not import a root count from another domain.
Do not start with 'this system needs 6 roots' because Backblaze had 6.
Do not design a verdict system before completing Step C.
Do not claim amplifier behavior before completing Step C.
Do not discuss CL-05 correction before completing Step C.

upper_limit_guidance.source: ChatGPT empirical discipline rule, consistent with ROOT-001 eigenvalue analysis.
upper_limit_guidance.guideline: 3-4 roots: minimal workable monitoring core. 5-6 roots: strong structural definition for a complex domain. 7-9 roots: only if data clearly supports extra independent dimensions. 10+ roots: suspicious unless genuinely independent and decision-relevant.
upper_limit_guidance.stopping_rule: At what point does the next root stop adding structure and start adding redundancy or noise? That is the real stopping rule.

what_done_looks_like.description: A completed HUF domain onboarding produces:
deliverables: 1. Carrier definition — substrate, normalization, named field elements
2. Panel description — domains/sites, time periods, snapshot count, missingness rules
3. Metric library — full list computed on every snapshot
4. Root map — correlation clusters and chosen representatives
5. Dimensional summary — how many dimensions for 95% and 99% variance
6. Polarity table — which direction means healthier, tested against domain ground truth
7. Structural vs operational split — what belongs in monitoring vs outcome evaluation
8. Identity check results — separate from scalar root scores
9. Minimum deployment set — the smallest root set that is empirically justified

the_safety_rule.statement: CL stack commitment (CL-01 through CL-04) can proceed after Step B. Domain-specific root-count commitment requires completion of Step C. No exceptions.
the_safety_rule.rationale: Basic monitoring (admissibility, drift, complexity) is portable and safe. Root architecture is domain-specific and must be earned from data.

backblaze.step_A: COMPLETE. Carrier = drive model share vector per datacenter.
backblaze.step_B: COMPLETE. TV drift, Shannon-Keff, identity tracking all operational.
backblaze.step_C: COMPLETE. ROOT-001 found 9 roots at r>0.90, eigenvalue analysis gives 4 dims for 95%, 6 for 99%. RCA-001 proved polarity alignment. CD-007 proposes 6-root architecture with 2 verification roots.
backblaze.status: Empirically strong, architecturally provisional (per ChatGPT validity review).

ramsar.step_A: IN PROGRESS. Carrier = site area share vector per national portfolio. Croatia used as first admissibility and extraction case.
ramsar.step_B: NOT STARTED. Requires multi-country, multi-cycle panel.
ramsar.step_C: NOT STARTED. Requires completion of Step B.
ramsar.status: CL-01 admissibility and first Keff extraction underway. Root commitment premature.

esa_planck.step_A: NOT STARTED. Carrier definition is the research question. Candidate substrates: power spectral density across multipole bands, pixel intensity distribution across sky regions, or component separation shares.
esa_planck.status: Pre-Step-A. Data available (353 GHz sky maps, PSO catalog). Carrier definition required before any HUF work.
